task_name: Non-default whole-file create and overwrite writes
goal: Fix the final Phase-B write adapter blocker: BP-141 can reach the sandbox real-write trigger and Maya corridor, but new-file writes land in SmartPush's overwrite bucket, which currently blocks non-default targets. Add a direct GitHub contents API whole-file write for non-default targets that can create or update a file, and route non-default overwrites through it. Keep the default soulmatch overwrite path unchanged and do not modify gates, profiles, endpoints, or orchestrator core.
scope_out: Do not modify target profiles, the sandbox real-write endpoint, server dispatch, Maya gate client, providers, orchestrator core, repo index, or deploy settings.
Do not change the default soulmatch overwrite path; it must still use the existing /push dispatch.
Do not bypass or alter the Maya corridor.
Do not widen target selection or introduce new repo defaults.
Do not log, return, or commit any token value.
Do not perform a live GitHub write during implementation or tests.
claims: opusPatchMode.ts exports putFileContent, a whole-file write helper using the GitHub contents API.
putFileContent GETs the target file first; if it exists, it includes sha in the PUT body, and if GET returns 404, it omits sha so GitHub creates the file.
SmartPush routes non-default overwrites through putFileContent when a GitHub token is available.
SmartPush reports a clear missing-token error for non-default overwrites when no GitHub token is available.
SmartPush default target overwrites remain on the existing /push path.
Tests prove create-vs-update payload shape, non-default routing, missing-token behavior, and unchanged default routing without network writes.
assumptions: BP-140 is merged: SmartPush already has a parsed targetRepo and target-aware direct patch path.
BP-141 is merged: the guarded sandbox real-write trigger is live, fixed to the sandbox and one target file.
The live blocker was the SmartPush non-default overwrite branch, not Maya corridor or target routing.
The GitHub contents API supports create when sha is omitted and update when sha is included.
dod: putFileContent creates a new file by omitting sha after a 404 GET, proven by test.
putFileContent updates an existing file by including sha after a successful GET, proven by test.
smartPush non-default overwrite calls putFileContent with the target owner/name and no longer returns overwrite push unsupported for non-default target, proven by test.
smartPush default overwrite still uses /push and does not call putFileContent, proven by test.
Source check: no changes to sandboxRealWrite, builderTargetProfiles, opusTaskOrchestrator, Maya gate client, providers, or server dispatch.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
stop_conditions: A live write would be required during implementation or tests.
Default soulmatch overwrite behavior cannot be preserved.
Orchestrator, profile, endpoint, provider, or Maya gate changes become necessary.
The GitHub token would need to be logged, returned, or committed.
The non-default write path cannot be tested without network writes.
prior_task_findings: BP-141 safely exposed the guarded sandbox real-write trigger. Live Phase-B testing reached the final adapter blocker: non-default overwrites/new-file writes were rejected with overwrite push unsupported for non-default target. The target and corridor were already correct; only the whole-file write adapter needs to support non-default repos.
